output.title: Landlord Withholding Your Security Deposit? Here's How to Get It Back
output.meta_description: Is your landlord withholding your security deposit? Learn your tenant rights, what landlords can deduct, and how to send a demand letter in 60 seconds for $19.99.
content_outline: The Problem: When Landlords Withhold, Delay, or Refuse to Return Deposits
Know Your Rights: Is Your Landlord Required to Return Your Deposit?
Can a Landlord Keep Your Security Deposit? Legal Deductions vs. Illegal Holds
State-by-State Timelines: How Long Before a Landlord Must Return Your Deposit
What to Do When Your Landlord Won't Give Your Deposit Back
Writing a Security Deposit Dispute Letter That Gets Results
Escalation Options: Small Claims Court and Beyond
Get Your Money Back in 60 Seconds Without a Lawyer
key_points: Most U.S. states require landlords to return security deposits within 14–60 days and provide an itemized list of any deductions
Landlords cannot legally withhold deposits for normal wear and tear or make deductions without written documentation
A formal demand letter is often the fastest, most effective way to recover a withheld deposit without hiring an attorney
Deposits-Back.com lets tenants generate a professional deposit return demand letter in 60 seconds for a flat $19.99 with no sign-up or subscription required
output.cta: Stop waiting and start fighting back. Create your deposit return demand letter in 60 seconds at Deposits-Back.com for just $19.99—no sign-up, no subscription, no lawyer needed.
output.target_audience: Frustrated US tenants whose landlords are withholding, delaying, or wrongfully deducting from their security deposit and need a fast, affordable way to recover their money
